symptoms.symptom_name: Broad-based unsteady (ataxic) gait, unable to walk safely without assistance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive ataxia in a gluten-sensitive patient after exposure supports gluten ataxia.
Acute ataxia is a classic stroke mimic and cannot distinguish gluten ataxia from cerebellar stroke without examination/imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical ascending burning numbness in both feet to the ankles (distal sensory neuropathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A symmetric distal burning sensory pattern is typical of a length-dependent peripheral neuropathy.
Ascending sensory symptoms overlap with early GBS; motor/respiratory screen is needed to distinguish.
Cognitive slowing is reported with gluten-related neurological disease.
Acute cognitive change is non-specific and must not be assumed benign; contributes to the need for stroke exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: High falls risk with functional mobility impairment (two-person assist required)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe ataxia with inability to walk unaided and environmental hazards constitutes a high, immediately actionable falls risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved proximal muscle strength; no preceding severe viral illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved proximal power and absence of a preceding illness lower GBS probability, though progression must be monitored.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetrical deficits (both feet, whole-body balance), not one-sided

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetry favours a diffuse/immune process over a focal stroke, but does not exclude an atypical posterior-circulation stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: No facial droop, slurred speech, dysphagia, or diplopia (no lateralising/bulbar signs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of lateralising/bulbar features lowers stroke probability but cannot exclude it without examination and imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual onset over ~48 hours, not sudden

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gradual onset is somewhat less typical of stroke, but stroke can be stuttering/progressive, so this only weakly lowers probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Broad-based ataxic gait, positive Romberg, distal symmetric pinprick loss to the ankles, diminished ankle reflexes, cranial nerves grossly intact, no unilateral weakness (in-person examination findings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ataxic gait with positive Romberg, distal sensory loss, and reduced ankle reflexes fit combined cerebellar/sensory involvement. Clinician-only findings sealed from the AI-Doctor-readable layer.
Symmetric findings with intact cranial nerves and no lateralising weakness are reassuring but do not replace imaging; not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral symmetry, no lateralising/bulbar signs, and gradual onset together lower stroke probability but are insufficient to exclude it via telehealth; urgent in-person assessment/imaging remains required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person neurological assessment and neuroimaging (CT/MRI) to exclude posterior-circulation stroke
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Acute ataxia/cognitive change cannot be safely attributed to gluten disease without excluding stroke, which requires examination and imaging telehealth cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Nutritional/neuro-immunology bloods (B12, folate, iron, vitamin E, coeliac serology)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies treatable nutritional causes of ataxia/neuropathy and confirms gluten-related autoimmunity; part of the in-person work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Falls risk and home-safety (occupational therapy) assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Addresses the immediate, actionable high falls risk with two-person assist and environmental hazards.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: High-dose vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) supplementation for the neuropathy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Pyridoxine at high/prolonged doses can cause or worsen sensory neuropathy; it should not be recommended for this burning neuropathy.
